elements.sent-2411-001: In this work, we present a general Milstein-type scheme for McKean--Vlasov stochastic differential equations (SDEs) driven by Brownian motion and Poisson random measure and the associated system of interacting particles where drift, diffusion and jump coefficients may grow super-linearly in the state variable and linearly in the measure component.
elements.sent-2411-002: The strong rate of $\mathcal{L ^2$-convergence of the proposed scheme is shown to be arbitrarily close to one under appropriate regularity assumptions on the coefficients.}

elements.sent-2411-004: Moreover, we use the notion of Lions derivative to examine our results.
elements.sent-2411-005: The two-fold challenges arising due to the presence of the empirical measure and super-linearity of the jump coefficient are resolved by identifying and exploiting an appropriate coercivity-type condition.

elements.sent-2411-011: For a fixed constant $ T>0$, consider the McKean--Vlasov stochastic differential equation (SDE) driven by Brownian motion and compensated Poisson random measure as given below, \begin{equation}\label{eq:sde} x_t=x_0 + \int_0^t b(x_s, \mu_s^x)ds + \int_0^t \sigma(x_s,\mu_s^x)dw_s +\int_0^t\int_Z \gamma(x_s,\mu_s^x,z)\tilde{n}_p(ds, dz)\end{equation} almost surely for any $t\in [0, T]$, where $ \mu^x_t$ denotes the law of $x_t$.
elements.sent-2411-012: The defining characteristic of McKean--Vlasov SDEs is the presence of the law $ \mu^x_t$ in the coefficients, which necessitates further investigation beyond that for classical SDEs.
elements.sent-2411-013: McKean--Vlasov equations such as \eqref{eq:sde and its special cases are widely used in different fields, including finance, biological sciences, neuroscience, statistical physics and machine learning, see \cite{Baladron2012, Bolley2011, Bossy2015, Carmona2018-I, Carmona2018-II, Carrillo2010, Chiara2017, Delarue2015, Dreyer2011, Goddard2012, Guhlke2018, Holm2006, Jin2020, Kolokolnikov2013, Mehri2020, Ullner2018 and the references therein.}}
elements.sent-2411-014: Even more so than classical SDEs, McKean--Vlasov SDEs too are often difficult to solve explicitly, as the law $ \mu^x_t$ is typically unknown, and this has led to keen interest by researchers in constructing numerical solutions of such equations.
elements.sent-2411-015: Broadly speaking, most numerical schemes for McKean--Vlasov SDEs can be broken into two steps.
elements.sent-2411-016: First, the law $ \mu^x_t$ is approximated by the empirical law and then a time-discretization is performed.

elements.sent-2411-198: Then, for any $u,v\in\{1,\ldots,d\}$ and $x\in\mathbb R^d$, \begin{equation}\partial_{x^u}f(x) =p|x|^{p-2}x^u and \partial_{x^v}\partial_{x^u}f(x) =p(p-2)|x|^{p-4} x^u x^v +p|x|^{p-2} 1_{\{u=v\}}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-199: By Taylor's theorem for a $f\in \mathcal C^2(\mathbb R^d)$ \begin{equation}f(x)-f(y)-\sum_{u=1}^{d} (x^u-y^u)\partial_{x^u}f(y) =\sum_{u,v=1}^{d} (x^u-y^u) (x^v-y^v) \int_0^1(1-\theta) \partial_{x^v} \partial_{x^u} f\big(y+\theta(x-y)\big) d\theta\end{equation} which on taking $f(x)=|x|^p$ yields \begin{equation}|x|^p-|y|^p-p\sum_{u=1}^{d} (x^u-y^u) |y|^{p-2}y^u = p(p-2)\sum_{u,v=1}^{d} (x^u-y^u) (x^v-y^v) \int_0^1(1-\theta) \big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^{p-4} \big(y^u+\theta(x^u-y^u)\big)\big(y^v+\theta(x^v-y^v)\big) d\theta \quad+ p\sum_{u,v=1}^{d} (x^u-y^u) (x^v-y^v) \int_0^1(1-\theta) \big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^{p-2} 1_{\{u=v\}} d\theta = p(p-2)\int_0^1(1-\theta) \big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^{p-4} \Big(\sum_{u=1}^d (x^u-y^u)\big(y^u+\theta(x^u-y^u)\Big)^2 d\theta \quad+ p\sum_{u=1}^{d} (x^u-y^u)^2 \int_0^1(1-\theta) \big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^{p-2} d\theta \leq p(p-2) \int_0^1(1-\theta) \big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^{p-4} |x-y|^2\big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^2 d\theta \quad+ p |x-y|^2 \int_0^1(1-\theta) \big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^{p-2} d\theta =p(p-1) |x-y|^2 \int_0^1(1-\theta) \big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^{p-2} d\theta\end{equation} for any $x,y\in\mathbb R^d$.
elements.formula-2411-113: \begin{equation}|x|^p-|y|^p-p\sum_{u=1}^{d} (x^u-y^u) |y|^{p-2}y^u = p(p-2)\sum_{u,v=1}^{d} (x^u-y^u) (x^v-y^v) \int_0^1(1-\theta) \big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^{p-4} \big(y^u+\theta(x^u-y^u)\big)\big(y^v+\theta(x^v-y^v)\big) d\theta \quad+ p\sum_{u,v=1}^{d} (x^u-y^u) (x^v-y^v) \int_0^1(1-\theta) \big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^{p-2} 1_{\{u=v\}} d\theta = p(p-2)\int_0^1(1-\theta) \big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^{p-4} \Big(\sum_{u=1}^d (x^u-y^u)\big(y^u+\theta(x^u-y^u)\Big)^2 d\theta \quad+ p\sum_{u=1}^{d} (x^u-y^u)^2 \int_0^1(1-\theta) \big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^{p-2} d\theta \leq p(p-2) \int_0^1(1-\theta) \big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^{p-4} |x-y|^2\big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^2 d\theta \quad+ p |x-y|^2 \int_0^1(1-\theta) \big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^{p-2} d\theta =p(p-1) |x-y|^2 \int_0^1(1-\theta) \big|y+\theta(x-y)\big|^{p-2} d\theta\end{equation}
